content: Coming soon — a deep dive into the architectural decisions behind building high-throughput event streaming systems, including when to choose Kafka vs. Pub/Sub, partitioning strategies, and exactly-once semantics at scale.
summary: When you’re processing millions of events per second, every architectural choice is a trade-off. Here’s how I think about them.
title: Architectural Trade-offs in High-Throughput Event Streams

content: Coming soon — reflections on building privacy-first technical frameworks, turning compliance requirements into reusable engineering patterns, and why ‘privacy by design’ is more than a buzzword.
summary: Regulation isn’t a constraint — it’s a design requirement. Lessons from building privacy frameworks at Google.
title: Privacy by Design: Lessons from EU DMA Compliance

title: The Soft Side of Tech Lead: Managing 150M User Migrations

content:
Written 2018 ICIoTCT 2018 · Elsevier-SSRN A Hénon Map based Cryptosystem for VQ based Compressed Video Data Vandana Rathore, Ashish Verma, Aashish Kumar, Arup Kumar Pal
Proposes a video cryptosystem built on top of Vector Quantization (VQ) based compression. I frames are compressed via codebook generation, while P and B frames are handled through a motion estimation scheme without compensation. The compressed data is then encrypted using a 2-D Hénon chaotic map, yielding high entropy in encrypted frames and no perceptible information leakage.

summary:
Written

2018 ICIoTCT 2018 · Elsevier-SSRN

A Hénon Map based Cryptosystem for VQ based Compressed Video Data

Vandana Rathore, Ashish Verma, Aashish Kumar, Arup Kumar Pal

Proposes a video cryptosystem built on top of Vector Quantization (VQ) based compression. I frames are compressed via codebook generation, while P and B frames are handled through a motion estimation scheme without compensation. The compressed data is then encrypted using a 2-D Hénon chaotic map, yielding high entropy in encrypted frames and no perceptible information leakage.
title: Research Papers
